scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope beauty and wellness reporting produced explicit metric definitions, source precedence, caveats, and action thresholds grounded in booking, POS, package, retail, review, and licensure-aware records.
Denominator ambiguity for utilization was blocked until the tenant definition and source hierarchy were clarified.
Stale commission, inventory, complaint, and review extracts triggered a low-confidence escalation instead of a smoothed trend claim.
Booking, POS, accounting, and inventory disagreements were preserved as a reconciliation case with no single authoritative metric asserted.
The lane refused to turn short-window beauty-performance changes into a causal retention or review claim and identified the evidence gaps needed for stronger inference.
Unsupported public claim and testimonial language was blocked and routed to marketing-compliance and legal review with substantiation gaps named explicitly.
A generic KPI request was rejected cleanly so service-line, provider, licensure, and review context did not disappear behind a cosmetic industry label.
Medical-spa continuation, provider-scope, and complaint-escalation authority were routed out of lane without improvised operational or clinical judgment.
Missing clinic-status, licensure, and incident-resolution facts prevented unsupported site comparability and forced escalation.
Tenant-specific mixed-model metric adaptation was recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline definition and comparison posture.
Conflicting review, complaint, refund, and inspection signals were preserved as a conflict case rather than translated into a confident reputation or customer-experience narrative.
Unsupported benchmark, investor, and social-disclosure language was downgraded to an internal-only analytical note with missing methodology and approvals called out.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to beauty and wellness metrics, booking and POS truth sets, review and testimonial controls, med-spa boundary awareness, and disclosure-safe reporting.

Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and both cross-industry abilities required to keep this pack from drifting into generic BI or claim-signoff behavior.
